question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildContractCreateTransactionBody()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a negative estimatedGas value into buildContractCreateTransactionBody(), causing the gas field in ContractCreateTransactionBody to be set to a negative long, which may be interpreted as a very large unsigned value by the executor, authorizing near-infinite gas at the fixed CONTRACT_CREATE_TX_FEES fee outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getSenderAccountID()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a sender address that resolves to an account with IMMUTABILITY_SENTINEL_KEY, causing getSenderAccountID() to complete the hollow account with DEFAULT_KEY in the write cache, and then use this completed account as the fee payer for a high-cost transaction, effectively enabling a previously immutable account to pay fees outside design parameters?
